{{- /* ASCII / box-drawing diagrams. Use a fenced ```ascii block: ```ascii ┌────────┐ ┌────────────────┐ │ Client │──TLS─▶ Server │ └────────┘ └────────────────┘ ``` The block is auto-dedented (the common leading indentation is stripped) and trailing spaces are trimmed; the inner .cs-ascii-art is then centred as a rigid unit inside the full-width frame by the CSS. So you can draw the diagram flush-left or indented for readability and never hand-pad it to centre. Rendered in a diagram-friendly 
 (tight line-height + a monospace
  stack with full box-drawing coverage) so connectors line up. Box-drawing
  chars pass through; any <, >, & are auto-escaped.
*/ -}}
{{- $lines := split (trim .Inner "\n") "\n" -}}
{{- /* smallest leading-space run across non-blank lines = the common indent */ -}}
{{- $min := -1 -}}
{{- range $lines -}}
  {{- $body := strings.TrimLeft " " . -}}
  {{- if ne $body "" -}}
    {{- $lead := sub (len .) (len $body) -}}
    {{- if or (lt $min 0) (lt $lead $min) -}}{{- $min = $lead -}}{{- end -}}
  {{- end -}}
{{- end -}}
{{- if lt $min 0 -}}{{- $min = 0 -}}{{- end -}}
{{- $out := slice -}}
{{- range $lines -}}
  {{- $out = $out | append (strings.TrimRight " " (substr . $min)) -}}
{{- end -}}

{{ delimit $out "\n" }}
